title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a Go poison-message remediation workflow with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go poison-message remediation workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Dead Letter Trigger Route
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 9721 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound dead-letter history, stop cycles, and separate retry tiers from authorized parking; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
